content.problemStatement:
You are given a sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč. An ordered pair of indices (i,j)(i,j)(i,j) in this sequence is called a beautiful pair if i‚Č†ji \neq jiÓÄ†=j and Ai‚ąíAjAi<Ai‚ąíAjAj\frac{A_i - A_j}{A_i} \lt \frac{A_i - A_j}{A_j}Ai‚ÄčAi‚Äč‚ąíAj‚Äč‚Äč<Aj‚ÄčAi‚Äč‚ąíAj‚Äč‚Äč. (Here, division represents real division, e.g. 52\frac{5}{2}25‚Äč is equal to 2.502.502.50.)

Help Chef find the number of beautiful pairs present in the given sequence.

**Note:** Since the input is large, prefer using fast input methods.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the number of beautiful pairs present in the given sequence.
